novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses Chernozhukov, Fernández-Val, Han, and Wüthrich (2024), arXiv:2403.05850, which uses local copula invariance for discrete/continuous treatments with binary IV; it does not subsume the rank-slice partial-ID slope kernel, but it is a closer IV-plus-copula comparator than several cited classics.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a related-work paragraph distinguishing the proposal from Chernozhukov-Fernández-Val-Han-Wüthrich local copula invariance: point ID of ATE/QTE under a binary IV versus partial ID of an adjacent-potential-outcome rank-slice slope under sensitivity and copula-tube restrictions.

State the two-bin witness as a named finite LP with explicit row/column margins, outcome cell values, and the signed IV moment inequality; this would make the Conjecture 1 sanity reduction auditable instead of only described in prose.

For Conjecture 2, derive or conjecture an explicit formula for the critical radius rho* in the two-bin witness, even if only under normalized margins; this would lift the phase-transition claim from qualitative to quantitatively field-level.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature comparison to include the local-copula IV paper, then tighten the two-bin witness algebra without changing the locked kernel.
